page: Microsoft Azure D192lds_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Dldsv7 family. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor and runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The instance is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU.

This server includes a large local storage capacity of 11339 GB and is Premium Storage capable. It does not include any GPU hardware, with a GPU count of zero. The hardware profile is designed for compute-heavy tasks that require substantial local disk space but do not require graphics acceleration.

Given the low memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, this instance offers a compute-optimized resource density. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that demand massive parallel processing power and high-capacity local storage without the overhead of a high-memory footprint. Appropriate workloads include large-scale batch processing, compute-heavy distributed systems, and storage-intensive databases that benefit from high vCPU counts and local disk capacity.

This hardware profile is designed for high-compute workloads requiring substantial local disk space but lower memory density. It is suited for large-scale batch processing, distributed compute tasks, and storage-intensive database workloads.
